title: Virgin Galactic Holdings Inc (SPCE) Q3 2024 Earnings Call Highlights: Navigating Cash Flow Challenges Amid Fleet Expansion
text: Virgin Galactic reported modest Q3 2024 results as the company continues investing heavily in fleet expansion. The spaceflight company generated just $402,000 in revenue from future astronaut membership fees while maintaining substantial operational costs. Operating expenses reached $82 million, representing an improvement from $116 million in the prior year period. However, capital expenditures increased significantly to $39 million from $13 million, reflecting accelerated manufacturing efforts. The company reported negative free cash flow of $118 million, slightly worse than the negative $105 million recorded in the same quarter last year. As of the earnings date, Virgin Galactic maintained $744 million in cash and marketable securities, providing a financial cushion for ongoing development activities. Management indicated the company has entered the manufacturing phase for its spaceship program. CEO Michael Colglazier stated the firm expects to open ticket sales when within a year of flying private citizens, targeting the latter half of 2025. The company projects Q4 2024 free cash flow between negative $115 million and $125 million, with expectations that spending will decline through 2025 as it approaches cash flow positivity with its first two operational Delta-class spacecraft.
